I'm happy to resubmit a new pull request to alleviate some of these concerns. My intuition on the finer points of `int`/`float` multiplication is not great, so I have some questions on how to accomplish this. 

1. How does moving the constant out of the parentheses cause a loss of precision for floating point inputs? I understand that `nd` is an `int` and `nd/(nd-1)` is being cast to a float internally. It's unclear to me what the practical difference between `(float)*float(int/int)` vs. `(float*int)/int` is.

2. Would adding a type check and performing the computation differently depending on the type be permissible? Or is this type of behavior frowned upon?

3. This passed the `polynomial` unit tests. Should there be new ones created to tease out these problems?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2498061636/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
